source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
This figure is made of squares. What is the side of the biggest square?
<image1>

image_dependency: question_text uses 'This figure', which explicitly refers to an image
the task asks for the side of the biggest square, a visual/spatial property of the figure
multi_step: the prompt concerns a figure made of multiple squares, so solving likely requires identifying relevant squares and comparing or combining side relationships
the target is the biggest square, which implies at least a selection/comparison step beyond simple lookup
exact visual structure is not provided in metadata, so complexity is scored conservatively
verifiability: registry_passed=true and the image asset exists
the requested quantity is concrete and, with the image, can be checked against the figure

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present, so the core semantic assets are intact
registry passed with no recorded asset issues
high image dependency is supported because the referenced figure image exists
verifiability is high because the expected answer is a specific numeric value
the only noted risk is missing image content description in metadata, which does not block recoverability when the image asset itself is available
